channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review including RCTs in people with MS (EDSS ≤6.0) found that physical exercise interventions may positively influence spasticity, though specific effects were narratively synthesized.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy was developed, categorized into three domains: functioning, participation, and health-related quality of life.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Oxford visual perception screen detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors: an early cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study.
item.description: The Oxford Visual Perception Screen (OxVPS) effectively detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute stroke survivors (within 6 weeks), aligning with guidelines for efficient identification.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: Mandatory bundled payments for lower extremity joint replacement (LEJR) under the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) model led to a significant decrease in hospitals providing LEJR prehabilitation services in the US Medicare population, impacting 62,217 beneficiaries.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke patients found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved functional exercise capacity, physical activity, and quality-of-life over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: This cross-sectional study found that multimodal integration of gait, balance, and infrared thermography data enhanced machine-learning classification of knee osteoarthritis (KOA) compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Effectiveness of Combined Biopsychosocial Interventions for Chronic Low Back Pain: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs evaluates the effectiveness of combined biopsychosocial interventions for chronic low back pain in reducing pain and functional disability.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The 2027 Proposed Rule is Out. Here's What You Need to Know.
item.description: The 2027 Proposed Rule for Medicare in the US has been released, outlining key changes that physical therapists need to understand regarding reimbursement and practice guidelines.
item.author: WebPT

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial
item.description: This randomized controlled trial investigates the long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain, a condition affecting 3% to 4% of adults worldwide.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model resulted in a 70.9% plan-of-care completion rate (exceeding national benchmarks of 43%), with 96% of completers achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres
item.description: A pioneering program has launched in UK rehab centers to test the ARC-EX system, a breakthrough spinal cord injury treatment combining targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises, aimed at restoring strength and movement.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials found no significant differences in the clinical effects of various non-thrust joint mobilization techniques on objective and subjective outcomes for individuals with shoulder adhesive capsulitis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke, particularly in the detailed comparison and description of rehabilitation protocols between contralateral C7 (CC7) nerve transfer and technology-assisted/high-dose rehabilitation approaches for upper limb impairment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found sarcopenia increases the risk of adjacent segment degeneration (ASD) within 3 years of anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) in 333 adult patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation (tDCS) Application in Neurorehabilitation: A Beginner’s Guide for Clinicians
item.description: This clinician's guide outlines transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) as a noninvasive brain stimulation technique that modulates brain excitability at subthreshold levels, highlighting its low cost (often <$1k) and ease of integration with rehabilitation therapy.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis investigated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, noting conflicting findings in existing literature.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Towards strengthening rehabilitation within health systems: a scoping review of rehabilitation-specific quality indicators
item.description: A scoping review identified, categorized, and appraised rehabilitation-specific quality indicators (QIs) with generic applicability for evaluating interprofessional rehabilitation care.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Transcutaneous spinal cord stimulation for stroke rehabilitation: a scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review identified transcutaneous spinal cord stimulation (tSCS) as an emerging noninvasive neuromodulation approach for stroke rehabilitation, with fragmented and methodologically heterogeneous evidence on its benefits.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of Pilates compared with home-based exercises for people with chronic non-specific low back pain: a trial-based economic evaluation.
item.description: A Brazilian randomized controlled trial found that Pilates was more cost-effective than home-based exercises for chronic non-specific low back pain, with comparable clinical outcomes, in 145 participants aged 18-50.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Acute Modulation of Lumbar Motor Unit Behavior During Experimentally Induced Low Back Pain: A Motor Unit Decomposition Analysis.
item.description: This study investigated the effects of experimentally induced acute low back pain on force steadiness and motor unit discharge characteristics during sustained spinal extension in 33 healthy participants, finding alterations in motor unit behavior.
item.author: PubMed
